div.head: 215. Editorial Note
div.p: The United States and the United Kingdom concluded an agreement on grain imports by an exchange of notes between Ambassador David K.E. Bruce and the Earl of Dundee, British Minister of State for Foreign Affairs, in London on April 15, 1964. The agreement entered into force on the same date. For text, see 15 UST 541; TIAS 5581. For Special Representative Herter’s statement of April 16 announcing the agreement, see Department of State Bulletin, May 4, 1964, pages 703–704. Additional documentation on the negotiations leading to this agreement is in Department of State, Central Files, INCO–GRAINS 17 UK-US.
